_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (va-legislature-refine runbook)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 Alaska House Republicans (Mat-Su/Eagle River delegation, HD24-28): Saddler, Johnson, St. Clair, Underwood, Moore. State tier. Every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence; flagged cross-party abortion deviations (Saddler, Moore).

profile.background: Eagle River/Chugiak Republican; b. 1961 Elyria, OH. BA journalism Miami (OH), MA journalism Ohio State; former newspaper reporter, PR exec, and Dunleavy-era DNR official. Served AK House Dist. 18 (2011-2018), returned in 2023 for Dist. 24. House Majority Leader 2023-2025; now in the all-Republican minority. Committees: Labor & Commerce, Resources. Reporting suggests he may retire/not seek reelection in 2026 (unconfirmed).

dan-saddler.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-17 (state tier). Mixed record with TWO documented cross-party abortion deviations: (1) 2023 voted to table/kill an amendment barring state Medicaid abortion funding (one of 5 Republicans joining Democrats); (2) 2026 voted YES on HB 195, which Alaska pro-life groups say expands chemical abortion (11 R crossed over). Conservative on crime (YES on SB 54 rolling back SB 91). Sustained Dunleavy's SB 140 education veto (charter-protection rationale). Stated support for voter ID and 2nd Amendment in candidate Q&A. Most social/federalism/immigration/gun cells: no recorded vote -> null (not inferred from party).

1.note: DEVIATION: voted to table an amendment barring state Medicaid abortion funding (2023) and voted YES on HB 195 (2026), which AK pro-life groups say expands chemical abortion. Documented votes contrary to abortion abolition/restriction.
Candidate Q&A: supports voter ID requirements ('same ID as required for cashing a check'), aggressive voter-roll purges, and pre-election (not same-day) registration.
Voted YES on SB 54 rolling back the SB 91 'catch-and-release' criminal-justice reform; campaigned against SB 91 as soft-on-crime.

profile.background: Palmer Republican; in the AK House continuously since 2017 (Dist. 11 -> 25). Elected House Minority Leader Nov 2025; former House Finance co-chair (2023-2024). Twice Mayor of Palmer; AAS Mat-Su College, BS Mathematics UAA. She and her husband home-schooled their five children.

delena-johnson.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-17 (state tier). On the clearest culture/fiscal votes she was on the conservative side: voted FOR the 2023 amendment to stop state-funded abortions (pro-life), and voted to SUSTAIN Dunleavy's SB 140 education veto (school-choice/charter camp). Home-schooled her five children. As Finance leader she backed balanced budgets and opposed means-testing the PFD ('the PFD is not a handout'), but is a reduced-PFD fiscal moderate rather than a full-statutory-PFD hardliner. No cross-party deviations documented. Interest-group ratings (Vote Smart/NRA-PVF/Alaska Right to Life) were not machine-accessible this session; cells with no found evidence left null.

1.note: Voted FOR the April 2023 budget amendment to stop state-funded abortions (named among the 16 Republicans on the pro-life side); documented anti-abortion-funding vote.

0.note: She and her husband home-schooled their five children (homeschool-freedom commitment); voted to sustain Dunleavy's SB 140 education veto, aligning with the school-choice/charter camp.

profile.background: Wasilla Republican; APPOINTED to AK House Dist. 26 by Gov. Dunleavy in Dec 2025 (sworn in Dec 30, 2025) to replace Cathy Tilton, who moved to the state Senate (~6-month record). 20-year Army career, retired Military Police First Sergeant; disabled veteran; MBA; 7 years as legislative aide to Cathy Tilton. GOP minority caucus; committees: Transportation, Community & Regional Affairs, State Affairs.

steve-st-clair.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-17 (state tier). ROSTER CORRECTION: appointed Dec 2025 (not elected 2024) to replace Cathy Tilton. Short tenure, so scored mainly from his own clear statements: 'Life begins at conception. This is a non-negotiable issue with me' (campaign Issues page); strong limited-government/budget-cutting record ('budget guy'; co-authored a plan to cut $1.5B; freeze state pay; pare Medicaid); 10th-Amendment framing ('If it is in the US Constitution it is a federal issue; all other issues belong to the state government'). No cross-party deviations found. Marriage, education, religious liberty, immigration, and gun specifics: no position located -> null.

0.note: Campaign Issues page (own words): 'Life begins at conception. This is a non-negotiable issue with me.'

2.note: Self-described 'budget guy'; co-authored United for Liberty plan to cut Alaska's budget by ~$1.5B over three years; supports freezing state-worker pay and paring optional Medicaid services. Documented opposition to deficit/over-spending.

3.note: Campaign Issues page (own words): 'If it is in the US Constitution, it is a federal issue; all other issues belong to the state government' -- 10th-Amendment / state-sovereignty framing.

profile.background: Wasilla Republican; first elected 2024 (defeated GOP incumbent David Eastman in the general). Realtor; former Mat-Su Borough School Board member (President 2023-2024); completed a non-denominational ministry program. GOP minority caucus; committees: Judiciary, Tribal Affairs, Education & Early Development.

jubilee-underwood.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-17 (state tier). Strong, well-documented social conservative: co-sponsor of HB 357, the Alaska Heartbeat Act (bans abortion after detectable heartbeat); endorsed by Alaska Family Action (pro-life). As Mat-Su school board president led the 2022 policy barring transgender students from bathrooms/locker rooms matching gender identity, and called transgender athletes in girls' sports 'a war on women.' Explicit own-words support for school choice ('allows parents to select schools that align with their own personal beliefs and provide a moral foundation'). Fiscal conservative (built two schools without debt; voted to sustain the HB 57 education-funding veto). No cross-party deviations.

0.note: Co-sponsor of HB 357 (Alaska Heartbeat Act); endorsed by Alaska Family Action (pro-life). Lists 'the sanctity of life' as a stated priority.

1.note: Active advocacy: co-sponsored HB 357, a heartbeat-based abortion ban with mandatory sonogram informed consent.

2.note: As Mat-Su school board president, voted in the 5-1 majority to bar transgender students from bathrooms/locker rooms matching gender identity (2022); called transgender athletes in girls' sports 'a war on women.' Affirms biological sex.

4.note: Touts being the first AK district to 'protect girls sports by creating policy that prevents biological boys from competing on girl's teams' -- opposes LGBTQ-identity promotion in schools.

0.note: Own words: 'School choice gives parents the freedom to select the best educational environment... schools that align with their own personal beliefs and provide a moral foundation.' Former school board member; parental-rights priority.

2.note: Own words: 'I support reducing our bloated budget and not overspend... I am Fiscally Conservative'; built two schools 'without incurring any debt'; voted to sustain Dunleavy's HB 57 spending veto. Documented opposition to deficit/over-spending.

profile.background: Northern/western Wasilla Republican; first elected Nov 2024 by a 9-vote ranked-choice margin (recount-confirmed). Real estate agent; lifelong Mat-Su Alaskan; married with six children. GOP minority caucus. In 2025-26 Mat-Su GOP district leaders moved to withdraw her endorsement over votes seen as siding with the Democrat-led majority (HB 57 education funding, SB 113 revenue/'Etsy Tax', and absence on an HB 53 PFD-cut amendment).

elexie-moore.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-17 (state tier). MIXED conservative record -- her own Mat-Su GOP district moved to pull her endorsement over deviations toward the Democrat-led majority. LIFE is genuinely split: she co-sponsored HB 357 (Alaska Heartbeat Act) and attended an Alaska Family Action pro-life event (affirms sanctity of life), BUT voted YES on HB 195 (May 2026, 32-8) which pro-life analysts say expands chemical abortion via pharmacist prescribing -- among 11 Republicans joining Democrats; an amendment to bar abortion-inducing drugs was tabled (documented deviation). FISCAL deviations: campaign 'no new taxes / full PFD' but supported SB 113 revenue bill and was absent for the HB 53 PFD-cut vote; voted FOR HB 57 passage then to sustain its veto. Own-words school-choice support (charters/vouchers). Marriage, justice, federalism, immigration, gun specifics: no position located -> null.

0.note: Co-sponsor of HB 357 (Alaska Heartbeat Act); attended an Alaska Family Action pro-life/pro-family gathering. Affirms the sanctity of life.

1.note: DEVIATION: voted YES on HB 195 (May 15, 2026, 32-8), which pro-life analysts say expands chemical abortion via pharmacist prescribing; an amendment to bar abortion-inducing drugs was tabled. Among 11 Republicans joining Democrats -- a documented vote contrary to abortion abolition (despite co-sponsoring HB 357).

0.note: Campaign site (own words): supports 'charter schools, vouchers, and other innovative solutions'; 'Parents are the primary decision-makers.'
